title: Ilysanthes anagallis
intro: Ilysanthes anagallis, also known as the African blue water lily or blue water lily, is a flowering plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its vibrant blue flowers and aquatic growth habit. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ilysanthes anagallis include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which have been isolated and studied for their potential therapeutic benefits. In traditional herbal medicine, Ilysanthes anagallis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Ilysanthes anagallis is classified within the family Gesneriaceae. This classification places it within the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants. Further, it belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, which is a subset of flowering plants with two- or four-chambered ovaries. At the kingdom level, Ilysanthes anagallis falls under Plantae, indicating its membership in the plant kingdom. Overall, its classification reflects its botanical characteristics and relationships within the plant world.
